channel.title: _Python_drafts on Pauls's Notes On Data & Software Engineering
channel.description: Recent content in _Python_drafts on Pauls's Notes On Data & Software Engineering

item.description:
Multi-Source Data Consolidation

Date: ___________
Author: ___________

Project Summary

This project consolidates 5 public UK economic datasets into a single decision-grade master table and dashboard, documenting all data quality decisions made during ingestion and transformation.

Data Sources

Source Series Format Frequency Coverage
Bank of England MIDAS Base Rate, Mortgage Approvals, SME Lending TSV Monthly 2000–
ONS Economy CPI, GDP, Unemployment, Earnings CSV Monthly/Quarterly 1988–
UK House Price Index Avg Price, HPI, Sales Volume CSV Monthly 1995–
FCA Complaints Complaints by firm/product CSV Bi-annual 2016–
FRED Gilt yield, GBP/USD CSV Daily/Monthly 1970–

Data Quality Decisions

Gaps & Nulls

  • Linear interpolation applied to gaps ≤ 3 consecutive months
  • Gaps > 3 months preserved as NaN with is_gap flag
  • [Document any series-specific decisions here]

Methodology Breaks

  • HPI pre-2005 flagged as mix-adjusted (not comparable to post-2005)
  • FCA complaint categories pre/post 2022 H1 crosswalk applied
  • GDP quarterly series forward-filled to monthly [rationale: ___]

Outliers

  • ±4 SD rolling 24-month cap applied to: [list series]
  • Anomalies verified against source before capping: [notes]

What Was Excluded and Why

Data Reason Excluded
Companies House bulk file Too large (3GB); sampled by SIC 6492
FCA data pre-2016 Taxonomy incompatible with current
[Add your own]

Known Limitations

  • Sales volume in HPI lags price data by ~2 months — not joined on same date
  • FRED CPI used for cross-validation only; ONS L55O is primary source
  • FCA firm names fuzzy-matched; match confidence < 0.85 excluded

How to Reproduce

  1. Download CSVs using links in notebooks/Multi_Source_Data_Consolidation.ipynb Section 1
  2. Place files in data/raw/
  3. Run notebook sections 2–4 in order
  4. Output dashboard at data/outputs/dashboard.html

Dependencies

pandas, numpy, plotly, requests, jinja2
pip install pandas numpy plotly requests jinja2
